_meta.purpose: Five hypothetical PE fund profiles for live demo of nav_stress_model.
_meta.calibration: Sector weights are constructed to span the realistic range of 2024-2026 European power-electronics PE strategies. NAV figures are illustrative.
_meta.disclaimer: All funds are fictional. Any resemblance to actual funds, GPs, or portfolio companies is unintentional and immaterial.

funds.name: Energy Transition Capital II
funds.strategy: Pan-European EV charging infrastructure, battery cell makers, and grid-storage power electronics
funds.narrative_for_demo: Tier-1 climate sponsor; high vintage-2022 entry valuations on the EV side now under pressure. Strong policy tailwind but heavily exposed to EV consumer cycle.

funds.name: Hyperscale Infrastructure Continuation Vehicle
funds.strategy: Data-center power infrastructure (PSU, UPS, liquid-cooled rack power) serving the AI build-out
funds.narrative_for_demo: GP-led CV recycling 2018-vintage data-center assets at marked-up NAV. Hyperscaler concentration risk: top-3 customers >80% of revenue across portfolio.

funds.name: EV Supply Chain Specialist III
funds.strategy: Concentrated bets on EV powertrain Tier-1 suppliers (inverter modules, OBC, SiC device makers)
funds.narrative_for_demo: Specialist GP, third fund in series. Extreme sector concentration is the strategy. NAV facility against this is the canonical test case for sub-sector underwriting.

funds.name: Diversified European Industrial Tech Fund
funds.strategy: Generalist European industrial technology with secondary exposure across all four power-electronics sub-sectors
funds.narrative_for_demo: Classic industrial-tech generalist. On paper a 'safe' diversified portfolio, but sub-sector decomposition reveals less headroom than the single-bucket view suggests.

funds.name: Polimi Power Electronics Spinout Fund
funds.strategy: Early-growth European power-electronics startups across EV, DC power, and grid applications
funds.narrative_for_demo: Smaller specialist fund, technology-curious LP base. Balanced exposure across sub-sectors makes it the cleanest illustration of how the model behaves under a 'normal' tilt.
